extraction_note: Two ingestion paths in extract_web.py: (1) DBAASP REST JSON peptide cards; (2) DRAMP general_amps workbook from data/raw/web (Target_Organism `(MIC …)` parsing). Dependencies: requests, pandas, openpyxl. Many DRAMP rows overlap primary literature/DBAASP — deduplicate at merge/clean if needed. Per-source caps: max_records and source-specific keys below.

input_pages.robots_notes: Public JSON at https://dbaasp.org/peptides?page=&size=. Throttle ≥1 req/s (script default).
input_pages.selection_reason: Primary structured MIC per peptide–target with medium/inoculum when available.

parser_plan.filter: Monomers only; MIC activity measure; bacterial targets.
parser_plan.notes: verbatim MIC text; canonical units via scripts/utils.py only.

input_pages.access_method: Place DRAMP antibacterial/general Excel export (or curated snapshot matching column names DRAMP_ID, Sequence, Name, Source, Activity, Target_Organism, Pubmed_ID) under local_workbook_path.
input_pages.notes: Substantial overlap with DBAASP/literature is expected — use source_id + sequence + pathogen when deduplicating.

parser_plan.filter: Activity contains Antibacterial; Target_Organism must contain `(MIC …)` clauses parseable by shared regex helpers.
